channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: OpenAI’s own model went rogue before Kimi had Wall Street sweating
item.description: Chinese AI lab Moonshot’s open model Kimi went viral this week for reasons that had less to do with the model itself and more to do with how the U.S. AI industry reacted to it. Meanwhile, an unreleased OpenAI model wandered outside its test environment and ended up connected to a real security breach at Hugging Face — a reminder […]

item.title: Your phone may not be as waterproof as you think — here's why IP68 can be misleading
item.description: Don't just jump straight into a pool with your phone, you savage.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 VS Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra : quelles différences ? Notre comparatif
item.description: Quelle différence peut-on observer entre les deux nouveaux modèles Samsung, le Galaxy Z Fold 8 et le Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra ? Réponse dans ce comparatif complet.

item.title: Batteries en rupture, factures salées et attente interminable : le cauchemar des réparations chez Cowboy
item.description: Depuis des mois, des propriétaires de vélos électriques Cowboy attendent une pièce, un capteur ou une batterie que seule la marque peut fournir. Nous avons interrogé trois utilisateurs, un réparateur agréé parisien et Grégory Trebaol, le patron de Rebirth qui a repris la marque en décembre, pour comprendre d'où vient ce blocage.

item.title: Sam Altman’s biometric startup World raises $52.5M via crypto sale
item.description: Sam Altman's side project — which seeks to scan the world's eyeballs and turn them into unique digital identifiers — has raised some fresh cash through a crypto sale.

item.title: The Anbernic RG SP looks like an awesome update to one of the most iconic Game Boys of all time
item.description: The Anbernic RG SP has pretty much everything you want from a modern take on the Game Boy Advance SP.

item.title: Peptide drugs popular with influencers get backed by FDA panel, despite warnings from scientists
item.description: Government scientists have warned that the substances haven’t proven to be safe or effective. A panel of federal health advisers on Thursday narrowly recommended easing access to several peptides popular with wellness influencers and celebrities, despite warnings from government scientists that the chemicals haven’t been shown to be safe or effective.In a series of votes, outside experts to the Food and Drug Administration advised the agency to drop current restrictions barring pharmacies from producing injectable peptides with names including BPC-157, TB-500 and KPV.The panel’s vote is not bi...

item.title: The new Lola x Miffy digital camera is extremely cute and ships with a themed wrist charm
item.description: Let's be honest we mostly wrote about this because we think it's really cute.

item.title: Meta now has free verification for Facebook users, as long as they’re willing to upload a video selfie
item.description: The new feature, which is separate from the paid blue badges, comes one day after Google launched a selfie-based option for signing in. Your face can now earn your Facebook account a verification badge.

item.title: GPT-Live, le mode vocal surpuissant de ChatGPT, débarque sur Windows et macOS
item.description: Environ 2 semaines après son annonce, GPT-Live s'invite sur les ordinateurs via l'application ChatGPT pour un contrôle total de la machine et d'agents IA à la voix.

item.title: As US weighs response to Chinese AI, industry urges against broad open-weight restrictions
item.description: AI companies, including Nvidia and Mistral, urge policymakers to avoid broad restrictions on open-weight AI models as Washington debates responses to Chinese AI and alleged model distillation.

item.title: GitHub restructures bug bounty program following flood of AI-generated reports
item.description: GitHub splits off open, public bug bounty programs from invite-only, VIP scheme which pays around 3-4x more.

item.title: Subway hit with another franchisee bankruptcy in just 2 months
item.description: A North Dakota operator with multiple Subway restaurants has filed for bankruptcy, raising questions about what happens to its locations next. A second Subway Sandwich Shop franchisee with multiple stores has filed for bankruptcy in as many months. This time, the Subway franchisee is a multi-store operator in North Dakota. Here’s what you need to know about the bankruptcy and its impact on Subway stores....

item.title: Amazon Prime Video devient une plateforme de cloud gaming : ce que ça signifie pour les abonnés
item.description: Des films, des séries et maintenant des jeux vidéo ! Amazon a intégré sa plateforme de cloud gaming Luna dans sa plateforme SVoD Prime Video pour ses abonnés aux États-Unis et au Royaume-Uni. À l'instar de Netflix, Prime Video entame un virage vidéoludique.

item.title: The Razer Kiyo V2 is an excellent webcam, even though its fancy new AI features aren't really that exciting
item.description: Razer's second-generation Kiyo webcam delivers powerful performance with an excellent physical design,

item.title: DJI Mavic 4 Pro : remise inédite de 220 euros sur le drone grand public le plus complet du moment
item.description: Si vous voulez ce qu'il se fait de mieux en matière de drone grand public, le DJI Mavic 4 Pro avec sa triple caméra Hasselblad de référence lancé en mai 2025 semble tout désigné. Surtout qu'en ce moment, il est disponible pour 1 879 euros au lieu de 2 099 euros chez Joybuy.

item.title: Spotify's rumored song ID tool could upstage Apple Music and Shazam in one fell swoop, and solve my biggest song-saving headache
item.description: Spotify is tipped to launch a built-in song identifier tool to one-up Apple and Shazam.

item.title: Netflix and Prime Video plot AI-driven homepages, but users say they don't want 'software to think' for them
item.description: Both Amazon and Netflix are reportedly testing out plans to incorporate AI into the first streaming screen you see.

item.title: 'Science fiction that happened': experts explain why OpenAI's 'mind-blowing' cyberattack should worry us all
item.description: This might be our first true glimpse of how dangerous AI could be in the future in terms of making hacks much more commonplace.

item.title: « Ce chiffre n’est plus à jour » problèmes de SAV, retard des livraisons, Cowboy répond à notre enquête sur Rebirth
item.description: Suite à la publication de nos articles sur Rebirth / Cowboy, la marque bruxelloise Cowboy nous a personnellement contactés pour apporter des éléments de réponse complémentaires. En voici les extraits.

item.title: Which of your old cables are actually worth keeping?
item.description: Clean out that cable junk drawer every so often and keep only the things you actually need.

item.title: Ubisoft CEO suggests there are pros and cons to Sony's plan to end the production of discs, 'but I think it will not disturb the industry too much'
item.description: Ubisoft CEO Yves Guillemot doesn't think Sony's decision to retire the production of physical game discs will have a considerable impact on the industry.

item.title: Google DeepMind's Demis Hassabis calls for 'urgent action' during 'precious window before AGI arrives' — and it's all starting to feel a bit Skynet
item.description: Hearing one of AI’s leading builders warn about losing control makes the future feel increasingly Skynet-adjacent.

item.title: Rivian sues the US government for ‘full refund’ of Trump tariffs
item.description: The automaker joins a long line of companies seeking such refunds. In April, Rivian CFO Claire McDonough said she expected the company stood to reap a refund in the "tens of millions of dollars."

item.title: Tesla’s car door defect could lead to tougher rules for everyone
item.description: Tesla's electronic door handles that have been linked to several deaths could lead to tougher safety rules for the entire auto industry. In a notice published today, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said that complaints about Tesla's mechanical door release don't warrant a defect investigation. Instead, the issue is better addressed through a broader […]...

item.title: Bluesky’s AI assistant Attie expands into an open social research tool
item.description: Users can now ask Attie questions about news, trends, and conversations on Bluesky and other apps on the AT Protocol.

item.title: One of the best 2-in-1 Chromebooks is almost $200 off for today only
item.description: Most Chromebooks ship with only 8GB of RAM, which is enough for some people, but certainly not enough for me. Multitaskers who want more memory in their next Chromebook should check out Best Buy, which is discounting Acer’s Chromebook Plus Spin 514 for the remainder of the day. Originally $749 for the configuration with 12GB […]

item.title: Midjourney acquired the astrology app Co-Star
item.description: The AI lab Midjourney continues to expand its purview beyond image and video generation.

item.title: Home energy costs are soaring. Why does Trump want our appliances to be even less efficient?
item.description: The war in Iran, climate change, and data centers are all driving up energy prices. Experts say Trump’s policies are making things worse. In early July, the Trump administration took yet another step to roll back energy efficiency efforts: It proposed a rule that would make it harder for future administrations to set efficiency mandates for home appliances....

item.title: 'No changes to our logging or privacy policies' — Windscribe reassures users on Canada's Bill C-22 fallout
item.description: Windscribe has told users its logging and privacy policies will not change under Canada's Bill C-22, as the surveillance bill heads to the Senate this autumn.

item.title: One ring to rule them all? Oura Ring rival RingConn debuts its Gen 3 smart ring with an official Lord Of The Rings tie-in
item.description: The RingConn Gen 3 breaks cover, and it does so with an unusual partnership... or should that be Fellowship?

item.title: Boox is taking on Xteink with its own tiny e-reader
item.description: Boox's new Tile lineup will take on Xteink's popular e-readers that are small enough to stick on the back of an iPhone with MagSafe. As spotted by Good E-Reader, the first one is the Picco, which will feature a 3.97-inch front-lit, black-and-white e-paper display and expandable storage with an SD card slot. Boox confirmed to […]

item.title: Silicon Valley Is Completely Divided Over Chinese AI
item.description: The AI “startups” worth billions of dollars are raising alarm bells about Chinese AI. The smaller players have a totally different take.

item.title: This feminist translation of ‘The Odyssey’ is controversial. And it’s flying off shelves
item.description: As Christopher Nolan’s blockbuster movie is smashing box-office records this summer, ‘The Odyssey’ ebooks and audio books are seeing a spike as well. It’s The Odyssey effect: As Christopher Nolan’s blockbuster movie The Odyssey is smashing box-office records—the July 17 opening weekend took in $124 million in the U.S. ($260 million globally)—it’s also bringing back gladiator sandals, and having an effect on book sales of Homer’s classic, especially for one translation....
